name: Pontón Alto Reservoir Loop
description: Hike the moderate 5.3-mile Pontón Alto Reservoir Loop in Spain, offering reservoir views and historical landmarks.
region_intro: Waterfall hiking trails around Embalse del Pontón Alto offer diverse landscapes at the foot of the Sierra de Peñalara, part of the Guadarrama mountains. The region is characterized by the Eresma River feeding the artificial lake, surrounded by lush vegetation including Scots pine, holm oaks, and oaks. Hikers can expect mountainous terrain with varied elevation changes, providing a natural setting for exploring cascades and streams. Best waterfall hiking trails around Embalse del Pontón Alto The most popular waterfall hiking route is Cascada del Chorro, Puerto del Reventón y Cerro Morete circular desde La Granja de San Ildefonso — Parque Nacional de la Sierra de Guadarrama, a 10.1 miles (16.2 km) trail that takes 6 hours 46 minutes to complete, featuring a prominent waterfall and mountain pass. Another top favourite among local hikers is Chorro Grande Trail, La Granja – Chorro Grande Waterfall loop from Parque Natural de la Sierra de Guadarrama Norte, a difficult 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This route leads through the natural park, highlighting the Chorro Grande Waterfall. Local hikers also love the Monk's Cave – Chorranca Waterfall loop from La Granja, a 10.2 miles (16.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain to the Chorranca Waterfall, often completed in about 5 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around Embalse del Pontón Alto is defined by mountain views, the Eresma River, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes are challenging. The routes in Embalse del Pontón Alto are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Embalse del Pontón Alto's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many waterfall hiking trails are there around Embalse del Pontón Alto?
A: There are currently 18 waterfall hiking trails available around Embalse del Pontón Alto. These routes offer diverse landscapes at the foot of the Sierra de Peñalara, providing a natural setting for exploring cascades and streams.

Q: Are there any easy waterfall hikes in the area?
A: While most waterfall trails around Embalse del Pontón Alto are considered difficult, there is one moderate route available: the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1148152824/cueva-del-monje-y-cerro-del-puerco-circular-desde-la-pradera-de-navalhorno-parque-nacional-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Monje y Cerro del Puerco circular from La Pradera de Navalhorno&lt;/a&gt;. This 14.7 km trail offers a less strenuous option to enjoy the region's natural beauty.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit the waterfalls near Embalse del Pontón Alto?
A: The area around Embalse del Pontón Alto is particularly vibrant in springtime, making it an ideal time for leisurely walks and enjoying the waterfalls at their fullest. Autumn and summer are also recommended seasons for visiting and enjoying outdoor activities.

Q: Are there any circular waterfall routes around Embalse del Pontón Alto?
A: Yes, several waterfall routes are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232669346/cascada-del-chorro-puerto-del-reventon-y-cerro-morete-circular-desde-la-granja-de-san-ildefonso-parque-nacional-de-la-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Chorro, Puerto del Reventón y Cerro Morete circular from La Granja de San Ildefonso&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 16.2 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40390886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monk's Cave – Chorranca Waterfall loop from La Granja&lt;/a&gt;, which is 16.5 km long.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on waterfall hikes in this region?
A: Waterfall hikes around Embalse del Pontón Alto offer diverse landscapes, characterized by the Eresma River feeding the artificial lake, surrounded by lush vegetation including Scots pine, holm oaks, and oaks. You can expect mountainous terrain with varied elevation changes, providing stunning views of the Sierra de Peñalara and the Guadarrama mountains.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the waterfall trails here?
A: The waterfall trails around Embalse del Pontón Alto are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 570 reviews. Hikers often praise the beautiful mountain views, the presence of the Eresma River, and the serene pine forests that define the area.

Q: Are there any specific waterfalls highlighted in the trails?
A: Yes, some trails are specifically named after prominent waterfalls. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1231986" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro Grande Trail, La Granja – Chorro Grande Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt;, which highlights the impressive Chorro Grande Waterfall. Another route leads to the Chorranca Waterfall, known as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40390886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monk's Cave – Chorranca Waterfall loop from La Granja&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I combine a waterfall hike with other activities or attractions?
A: Absolutely! The Embalse del Pontón Alto area is close to the historic town of La Granja de San Ildefonso, allowing you to combine a nature excursion with a visit to the Royal Palace and its magnificent gardens. The El Robledo recreational area, adjacent to the reservoir, also provides a tranquil spot for relaxation and family outings.

Q: What is the typical duration for a waterfall hike in this region?
A: The duration of waterfall hikes varies depending on the route's length and difficulty. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232669346/cascada-del-chorro-puerto-del-reventon-y-cerro-morete-circular-desde-la-granja-de-san-ildefonso-parque-nacional-de-la-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Chorro, Puerto del Reventón y Cerro Morete circular&lt;/a&gt; can take approximately 6 hours and 46 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31578321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro Grande Waterfall – Chorro Grande Trail loop&lt;/a&gt; is shorter, typically completed in about 2 hours and 37 minutes.

Q: Are there any trails that feature both waterfalls and mountain passes?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232669346/cascada-del-chorro-puerto-del-reventon-y-cerro-morete-circular-desde-la-granja-de-san-ildefonso-parque-nacional-de-la-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Chorro, Puerto del Reventón y Cerro Morete circular from La Granja de San Ildefonso&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent route that features both a beautiful waterfall and a significant mountain pass, offering a diverse and challenging hiking experience.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on these waterfall trails?
A: The waterfall trails around Embalse del Pontón Alto often involve significant elevation changes due to the mountainous terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232669346/cascada-del-chorro-puerto-del-reventon-y-cerro-morete-circular-desde-la-granja-de-san-ildefonso-parque-nacional-de-la-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Chorro, Puerto del Reventón y Cerro Morete circular&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of over 960 meters, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40390886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monk's Cave – Chorranca Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt; involves about 590 meters of ascent.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Pontón Alto Reservoir Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is generally considered a moderate hike. While some sections feature easily accessible paths, its length of approximately 8.6 km makes it a good option for those with a basic level of fitness. It's a great way to enjoy the scenery without overly challenging terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features easily-accessible paths, suggesting a mix of well-maintained tracks and possibly some natural, unpaved sections. Given its location around a reservoir, you can expect relatively flat terrain with occasional gentle climbs, suitable for most hikers.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: As you hike, you'll encounter several notable spots. You'll get a beautiful View of the Embalse del Pontón Alto and the Casa de Santa Cecilia. The route also passes by the historic San Vicente Ferrer Preaching Rock and offers views of the Pontón Alto Dam itself.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Komoot route for the Pontón Alto Reservoir Loop typically starts and ends in a circular fashion, implying a designated trailhead or accessible starting point. While specific parking details aren't provided, areas around reservoirs often have parking facilities. It's advisable to check local maps or recent visitor information for the most precise parking locations near the Embalse del Pontón Alto.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other well-known paths. Significant portions of the loop follow sections of the Camino de Santiago Complutense, Ruta del Sureste, and Sendero de los Reales Sitios. You'll also find it overlaps with parts of the Senda del Embalse del Pontón Alto and the Camino Natural de la Cañada Real Soriana Occidental.

faqs.question: Is the Pontón Alto Reservoir Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in natural areas like this are dog-friendly, but it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Always carry waste bags and clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are typically the best seasons for hiking in this region, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended if hiking during those months. Winter might offer a different, quieter experience, but be prepared for colder weather.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail or visit Parque Natural de la Sierra de Guadarrama Norte?
faqs.answer: The Pontón Alto Reservoir Loop is located within the Parque Natural de la Sierra de Guadarrama Norte. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking on public trails within the Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations, which may include rules about camping, fires, and waste disposal. It's a good idea to check the official park website for any specific updates or seasonal restrictions before your visit.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see along the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific wildlife sightings for this exact loop aren't detailed, reservoir areas and natural parks like the Sierra de Guadarrama are typically rich in biodiversity. You might spot various bird species, small mammals, and possibly deer. Keep an eye out, especially during quieter times of day.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is a natural experience, the area around the Pontón Alto Reservoir is close to towns and villages. You can usually find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in nearby communities, such as La Granja de San Ildefonso or Segovia, which are popular tourist destinations.
